title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. glomerulans
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. glomerulans, commonly known as the lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the A. vulgaris plant, characterized by its rosette of leaflets and clusters of small, yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of A. vulgaris subsp. glomerulans include its ability to reduce menstrual cramps, ease symptoms of menopause, and support digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for women's health issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
